objects.name: Chinese Volt Typhoon APT Maintains Persistent Access in US Critical Infrastructure
objects.description: Mandiant's threat intelligence team has published new research documenting the continued operations of Volt Typhoon, a threat actor assessed with high confidence to be sponsored by the People's Republic of China. The group has maintained stealthy, persistent access inside multiple US critical infrastructure organisations â€” including energy utilities, water systems, and telecommunications providers â€” for periods exceeding 18 months in some cases. Volt Typhoon is characterised by its exclusive use of living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques, using built-in operating system tools and legitimate software to blend in with normal network traffic and avoid detection. The group appears to be pre-positioning for potential disruptive or destructive attacks rather than conducting active espionage. Affected organisations span energy, water, transportation, and telecommunications sectors.

external_references.description: Chinese Volt Typhoon APT Maintains Persistent Access in US Critical Infrastructure
